content:
Top Reason for Playing Star Atlas
   "To earn rewards and build my empire" and "To be part of the future of blockchain games" - both at 23%
  "To experience new economy and technology" followed behind at 17%

Secondary Reasons
   "To explore the universe and immerse myself in the story" - 14%
   "To connect with communities/others and collaborate" - 11%

Lesser Motivations
"I want to earn money to eat and pay rent" - 9%
"I have nothing better to do" and "I have no idea why I'm here" - both at 1%

SUMMARY
At a superficial level the results show that a player base that is mostly driven by innovation, ambition, and curiosity.

Looking deeper, we see that these motivations suggest that many players are looking to create something meaningful, connect with something larger than themselves, and leave an impact, whether in the game world or the broader world of technology and innovation.  ⁠No Access

@Dragonmother @⪛⦿⫺ZeSKK

author: [SAG] Swift42 [CODE]
content:
"Create something meaningful" and "have an impact" is imo very underestimated and even ignored by game creators today.
When I look e.g. at today's MMOs, the PvP zones are nearly always either instanced or reset after some time. Or time gated. Or simply a gank zone or a pointless hack and slash.
But when I look at one of my first MMOs, Dark Age of Camelot, the complete PvP zone (or RvR zone in this case) was 100% permanent.
You conquered a castle? Congrats, from now on this castle belongs to your guild and realm. Forever, if nobody conquers it back. There was no reset.
Also your realm got advantages if you held a castle. And because there were 3 realms, no realm could conquer everything. You always had to watch your back when you fought against one realm - you were never 100% safe, which was of course fun.
I never saw such a good system again and I played a lot of MMOs after Daoc. And Daoc is nearly 25 years old. (sigh, time flies).
tl;dr: Meaningful game systems with an impact are super important.

author: [SAI] Sim [STAR]
content:
⁠archived-foundation-room⁠
@Deleted User 

"totally not true" in your opinion AND for your experience

Gaming isn’t what it used to be. The average skill level is immensely higher than it was 10 years ago.
Speaking from personal experience, I hit Diamond in the early seasons of Apex when it was a bigger deal, and more recently, I reached Master, which is the highest rank (except for Predator, which is reserved for the top 500 players worldwide). I’ve earned the 20-kill badge twice (in lobbies with 60 players) and the 4k damage badge three times. I don’t know if you have experience with Apex, but believe me, it’s pretty challenging to get these legitimately these days.
I’ve taken part in Warzone tournaments (not at the highest tier, of course, but I’ve played with streamers and content creators). Across both PlayStation and PC, I think I’m well above 10k hours in FPS/TPS games. (I'm not flexing, there are tens of thousands of players better than me, I'm just proving that I know what I'm talking about)

I get where you’re coming from, but at the same time, you have no idea what would happen in ranked and tournaments without SBMM. It would just be so wrong. Not only would it affect the flow of a match, but it would also create an uncontrollable randomness from game to game.
For public matches, I’d agree that we could leave SBMM out. As I said, I’m neither for nor against it.

SBMM isn’t something you can simply decide for or against off the bat. First, think about the product, then release some adjustments based on data the team gathers during the first few thousand games. We can certainly talk about it just for fun, but it’s way too early right now.

Just my two cents..

author: odvb
content:
Hey @Chri.z , I was wondering if you’ve been keeping an eye on the EVE Online economy, particularly the recent surge in PLEX prices, which many attribute to the introduction of player-created skins as a big sink. Do you think there’s potential for Star Atlas to implement something similar, allowing players to create or personalize in-game items, perhaps through the Metacrafting system in SAGE especially when fleet command arrives?
I understand that a skin system is planned for the future, but could introducing player-created or customizable items through Metacrafting serve as a valuable shortcut, both for player engagement and economic growth? Or do you think it might be too difficult or complex to implement such a system effectively at this stage?

author: odvb
content: It’s a bit disheartening to see the current limitations of the Metacrafting system. It feels like there’s so much untapped potential. It would be fantastic to see more features added, like temporarily available unique items such as skins or special assets. Incorporating these could breathe new life into the crafting system and provide players with exciting, dynamic opportunities to create and collect valuable in-game items.

I understand that a skin system is planned for the future, but could introducing player-created or customizable items through Metacrafting serve as a valuable shortcut, both for player engagement and economic growth? author: [AEP] Mandalorian (Djin Djarin)
content: You can change it at chrome://settings/?search=Use+graphics+acceleration+when+available 700mb is pretty normal for a Chrome task. Chrome with 1 tab and a few plugins and you are very easy at 1 gb used after a few minutes.  Also the green leaf icon is normal. Chrome puts some things on efficiency mode.  Try this: 1. Press F12 to open Dev Tools. 2. Right-Click on the Reload button. 3. Select 'Empty Cache and Hard Reload'. 4. Press F12 again to close Dev Tools.  If no success: Try incognito mode or different browser.

content: $20k-$40k for a one-off program audit if it touches blockchain would be a reasonable average to use (if you're working with one of the tier 1 auditors, which would be recommended). author: 《VΛ》ValPhoenix [STAR]
content: According to @Xcode , the cancel button won't come back. (yes, I know, bummer) So currently your only chance is to get the missing resources and complete the craft. There is no other way. The current Starcomm issues make the problem worse, because (at least I) have SEVERE number switching problems in Sage. Currently I can't trust any number (I have to double check everything on EveEye)  - hopefully it gets fixed soon. This was the reason I stepped into the "incomplete deposit" trap, because Sage told me I have enough PAs to craft a Mamba, while in reality I only had 30%. So my crew was stuck until I had enough PAs.

author: Xap✨ [STAR]
content: It turns out that, like in my case, if the numbers shown in the inventory were wrong, you didn't catch it, you try to craft something, and you  put "max" for the amount to craft, like I always do, if those numbers shown were wrong, you are totally f**d. Now I have to transport the amount I've put, at my expense, to restart the craft. It will take ages for transporting it, all of this not for my fault. And it can't be canceled.

author: questionoak
content:
I came here to see if it was just me and it seems that everyone is seeing the same issues. I claimed a bunch of different items being crafted. As always I wait 30 seconds or more then hard refresh my page (I have been doing this since for the better part of a year). I then went and crafted a bunch of golden tickets (not saying) by clicking Max. I then went to the crafting page and saw a Deposit button. I deposited five times with no luck (each time  I also had to "trust" the wallet transaction). I then did another refresh this time using Developer tools and cleared all the session data.  I tried again and got the same result.  As an engineer, I thought maybe there is a rounding code issue with the Max button.  I bought some electromagnets from the marketplace, moved them to SAGE, and the deposit worked.
This is the bug. ---- Rounding error on the Max button. I was probably off by 1 electromagnet although I bought a fair number.

author: questionoak
content: I just did another crafting effort and clicked Max quantity. I really didn't think I could reproduce the error but yes it showed up again...

author: questionoak
content: Any thoughts on how to get my 600+ crew back? I am at a total loss of what to do here. I clicked the Max button to craft the electromagnets for the GTs and got the Incomplete Deposit. I am not sure what I did next but I bought a few thousand magnets and copper wires (to fix a rounding error?). I was unable to make it work after numerous Deposit attempts for the Incompete Deposit. It looked like I was way low on crafting ingredients (mostly copper wire). I bought 1,000,000 copper wire and went to dinner.  I came back and saw I now have twice as much copper wire as I need.  However, I still cannot get the crafting going or get my crew back. How are you guys getting around these bugs? I disbanded all my commander and capital fleets today  to craft the GTs and I guess all that is out the window. Can't craft GTs and can't go back to mining....

author: diegomtxUstur
content: Hello @SW4GN3R and @Chri.z you really did a great job on the economic side, but i have thinked about, and i think that economy needs just a little small adjustment; don't forget that last time we add RFR into the economy the SDU's price gone to the moon, because the SDU's supply was too low, and then the economy stopped; i love the idea to mantain some kind of gambling (through sdu scan)  inside the game, but actually, with SDU only obtainable through scanning, i think that the economy is too much unbalanced towards gambling side; so i think that we also need an SDU recipe for crafting it in order to increase SDU supply and to prevent that SDU's price will go too high, so preventing a new economy stop

author: ROME | Aegir
content: Just from a personal perspective, activity on most of the local markets will probably be considerably less than on the galactic marketplace. Bots are mainly active on the more singular purchases like ships instead of resources which have buy and sell orders in the millions.  The best use-case for "botting" local markets is probably to also intend to ship those resources to a different starbase and then sell them there if it's worth the fuel cost. To me that actually leads to a healthy distribution of resources across the galaxy and more opportunity for the average players to always be able to sell something locally.

author: [SAI] Sim [STAR]
content: The only thing I feel like saying is that automations have always been tolerated, approved, and even encouraged by everyone because they were made available to the community, ensuring equal access. Having bots in the market that are closed-source and clearly used by the same four wallets isn’t great. On the other hand, there’s the benefit of liquidity provision, which is useful—I agree. However, it’s undeniable that a bot takes away the motivation to participate in the marketplace in a 'fair' way; it makes the experience a bit frustrating because you can’t compete on equal footing.

author: [SAI] Sim [STAR]
content: Exactly, there’s a price up to which the bot will follow and outbid you, both for buying and selling. Basically, if you want to sell your asset and be the best ask, you have to significantly underprice it (the less liquid the asset, the easier it is to depreciate it). And if you want to be the best bid, you might have to double the bot’s base price, which updates at a speed close to having a listener on a webhook..

author: [HEIM] CapThor
content: I'd say we have a few things going for us RIGHT NOW to combat bots at local marketplaces and also some future mechanisms.  ---------------- RIGHT NOW. 1) there is a lot more local marketplaces, so if a bot wants to cover them all, thats 100x the transaction fees and RPC calls needed.  2) these bot makers haven't improved their bots at all. they were keeping mining ships in SCORE (when it was active).   updating the code to SAGE is a huge effort  ------ FUTURE  1) I'd like to see local marketplaces tx be gated by the player profile and exp system.  Then we can actually enforce some limitations that is not easily bypassed by spinning up new accounts  2) there are ways to diminish the visibility of bots on the BUYER side.  e.g. present only the top buy price so player dont see buyers at all.  e.g. the amazon route.  3) More player education.  Bots are only as annoying as you let them be.  Set your prices outside the range and they aren't an issue.  --- THOUGHTS  1) Players mostly don't care about buying from bots.  They mostly just buy from the lowest.  If players did care then the bots would be out of business.   Icons/avatars are only as good as a player willing to take a higher/price.  2) Players selling into the marketplace are the most impacted by marketplace bots. It can be frustrating to sell when you are constantly outbid. We really need to address this point, but honestly this affects every single market everywhere.    In WOW they had enough players that players would do the same thing and overbid each other.  Will players cry foul when being outbid by players and not bots? Probably.

author: odvb
content:
My gpt has no real answer
In SAGE, the transaction volume is quite high, with an average of around 1,000 transactions per day per player. Each transaction currently costs 0.000005 SOL, which may seem negligible at first glance. However, as SOL's price rises, these costs become more noticeable:
At $200 per SOL, this is $0.001 per transaction, or about $1 per day.
At $400 per SOL, it doubles to $2 per day.
At $600 per SOL, it becomes $3 per day.
While this may not seem significant compared to other MMO costs, it adds up, especially for smaller players who are actively engaging with the system.
For comparison, a typical MMO subscription fee ranges between $10 and $15 per month, which provides full access to the game without additional micro-costs for basic gameplay actions. While Star Atlas is designed with a Web3 profit model where players can earn from their efforts, the transactional structure could become a real barrier for smaller players if SOL’s price continues to climb.
This raises a critical question: How can we balance accessibility for new and smaller players while maintaining profitability?
Suggestions like enabling testnet modules for practice or introducing flat transaction fee systems like ATLAS Prime could help mitigate this issue. Otherwise, the game risks alienating potential players who feel like they can’t participate effectively due to high costs.
The Web3 model offers incredible opportunities for profit, but we must ensure that it remains inclusive and sustainable for players of all levels, especially as the ecosystem grows and evolves.

author: Buzzec
content: Yeah it has been pretty intense. I think our biggest problem right now is actually a software issue.  Labs assistant doesn't have dynamic priority fees or fees based on CUs.  So we just slam a flat fee and it either works or it doesn't. And if it does work it sits there until we change it likely overpaying by 1-10x.  Or it doesn't work and then fleets stop working and complaints start rolling in.

author: Buzzec
content: Is it possible that we could benefit from Firedancer in terms of fees? I mean, it might not reduce fees directly, but assuming the network can handle more transactions per second, could we lower the base/prioritization fees and still have transactions processed effectively? ( aka "Can we expect some improvements from these upgrades Solana is receiving?") On top of that, we can still expect ATLAS/SOL to help alleviate the transaction cost burden

author: [SAI] Sim [STAR]
content: Not in the immediate future.  Chain cant run faster then its slowest validators.  You would need a majority of validators to adopt Firedancer first, which would force the potato nodes to upgrade.  Once there is enough throughput then priority fees could be reduced.   But if that happens we might just see even more transactions on chain.  So the question is can Solana technology outpace its growth.  In the interim, Firedancer is HUGE for Solana security.   Running on a single validator system means that a bug in the agave client could have huge ramifications.  Firedancer as a 2nd source of truth is exponentially safer for the chain.   I would not be surprised if validators ran both Agave+Firedancer client with mandatory consensus between the two.

reply.preview: Not in the immediate future. Chain cant run faster then its slowest validators. You would need a majority of validators to adopt Firedancer first, which would force the potato nodes to upgrade. Once there is enough throughput then priority fees could be reduced. But if that happens we might just see even more transactions on chain. So the question is can Solana technology outpace its growth. In the interim, Firedancer is HUGE for Solana security. Running on a single validator system means that a bug in the agave client could have huge ramifications. Firedancer as a 2nd source of truth is exponentially safer for the chain. I would not be surprised if validators ran both Agave+Firedancer client with mandatory consensus between the two.

content:
1 Player (average 1000tx daily)
Normal Fees (1x)

1 Txn Cost: $0.00119 (at $238) / $0.00250 (at $500)
Daily Cost: $1.19 (at $238) / $2.50 (at $500)
Monthly Cost: $35.70 (at $238) / $75.00 (at $500)

3x Priority Fees
1 Txn Cost: $0.00357 (at $238) / $0.00750 (at $500)
Daily Cost: $3.57 (at $238) / $7.50 (at $500)
Monthly Cost: $107.10 (at $238) / $225.00 (at $500)

5x Priority Fees
1 Txn Cost: $0.00595 (at $238) / $0.01250 (at $500)
Daily Cost: $5.95 (at $238) / $12.50 (at $500)
Monthly Cost: $178.50 (at $238) / $375.00 (at $500)


This is quite expensive if you consider this is just to process your game. 

Argument coming against is "youll earn atlas in game". Sure thing, but you're also buying some of the most expensive game assets to play. 

For larger players this is no issue, the smaller plsyer you are, the worse this becomes

author: [SLY] Groove [STAR]
content:
Thing is, the Exit Warp instruction still exists. It's just bundled in with the next action. You avoid the base tx fee, but you're still paying priority fees on the extra compute (if you calc priority fees "properly").

And transactions are size limited, so you can only bundle so many instructions.

I'm sure there's opportunities for bundling, but they probably won't have a significant impact.

author: [HEIM] Groza
content: Impact Summit wasn't that long ago but I feel like parts have been largely forgotten.  I'd like to reiterate that having Fleet Command in UE5 absolutely blew my mind.  Fleet Command + Project Galia + Surge all in one unified UE5 game module is exactly what this game needs.   When I sat down at the live UE PCs at Summit and had the ability to flip between these game modes I knew this was the future.     Seeing the alignment of all 3 projects in person brought back a sense of nostalgia and wonder that I've not experienced in a game in a very long time, opening EverQuest, playing Wow for the first time, Diablo 2, x-com....  I support you on this path 100000%.

author: [SAI] Sim [STAR]
content:
You have to consider, guys, that this game currently has only a few players compared to the numbers it could potentially achieve. Would you play Warzone with 15 players instead of 150? Or Apex with lobbies of just 20 players? Or even classic browser games like Travian, Ogame, or Ikariam (sorry, I'm a bit old school) with only a handful of players?

Fleet Command will open up the Star Atlas brand to an entirely new audience — mobile gamers — who make up 50% of the current gaming market.
Sometimes, the issue isn't the game itself, but the lack of a critical mass needed to unlock its full potential.

I’m not referring to SAGE in its current state, but to what it could become, with ship configuration and combat included.

Making changes before something has reached its final form might be premature. Adjustment are more than welcome, of course!

author: Xcode [STAR]
content:
There are direct links to play and labs there already, but as a newcomer it's not obvious what the call to action is.  We understand the need to update or replace the staratlas.com site, and to funnel new users from there to our other major products / sites ( govern,  build, lore, based, etc) and all the products on play (marketplace, score, faction claims, marketplace, graphic novel, DAC, Crew, player profiles ). Not to mention the individual product categories / classes inside the marketplace.

We need to take every person that lands on the main site, share the vision of Star Atlas, and guide them to an aspect of the Star Atlas ecosystem that appeals to them in just a few moments.

author: ! ᚺEIM | VɪᴋɪɴɢᴇɴBᴇɴɴʏViking
content:
AI like GPT is a tool, and like any tool, its value depends on how it's used. It’s designed to provide answers and insights based on vast datasets, which can be incredibly helpful when used responsibly. Sure, it’s not perfect—but neither are humans.

The real power of GPT is how it complements human knowledge. It’s not about ‘knowing everything,’ but about being a resource to assist in research, creativity, or problem-solving. Closing channels isn’t the answer—teaching people how to leverage AI effectively is. It’s not about replacing expertise but enhancing it.

author: mikatachan | [ROME]
content:
@!  ᚺEIM | VɪᴋɪɴɢᴇɴBᴇɴɴʏ  also throwing in my 2 cents as a crypto native who's been on the team of an nft project. As @[AEP] Mendizo mentioned, it's not like shitcoins care about regulatory scrutiny or repercussions since they're mostly anon. they pay a fee, boom listed. easy decision when you're trying to pump your bags. a good number of other tokens you see listed are also either incorporated or reside in jurisdictions where they have more freedom to do so.

As long as ATMTA  reside and do business in the US, they don't have as much flexibility or as much idgaf as many other projects. even operating in a different country but having employees in the US puts a lot of liability on the project. at least imo, they'e been operating as best they can within the known guidelines given... which is very little

author: mikatachan | [ROME]
content: Also if the community got them listed, no, those "issues" wouldn't be gone. it's a measure of risk and risk management. If ATMTA ever gets sued by the SEC for the project being a security, everything will be scrutinized. They have to distance themselves and take action to not directly promote, promise value to the token/holders, or otherwise fail the howey test.

author: [Peace DAC] TGI LizardMUD
content: I had a call with the founder of Paladin.  They are building a protocol called "P3".  It's a high priority port for users that are paying very high fees for transactions.  It bypasses JITO + Normal priority fees.  I asked him if he knew about Star Atlas, because I could see some colabs  for high priority TX. He didn't think it would work because he thinks Star Atlas needs large amounts of fast low tx, not high priority fee tx.   I said... actually.  People would pay a lot for the tX that really matter.  Transporting, crafting,  COMBAT.    We have already talked about people paying for faster TX for combat.  So that's a thing, and its already here.  Just waiting for the connections.  And then I think back to the degens we have... I could see people using P3 for scanning.  Degens.

reply.preview: I had a call with the founder of Paladin. They are building a protocol called "P3". It's a high priority port for users that are paying very high fees for transactions. It bypasses JITO + Normal priority fees. I asked him if he knew about Star Atlas, because I could see some colabs for high priority TX. He didn't think it would work because he thinks Star Atlas needs large amounts of fast low tx, not high priority fee tx. I said... actually. People would pay a lot for the tX that really matter. Transporting, crafting, COMBAT. We have already talked about people paying for faster TX for combat. So that's a thing, and its already here. Just waiting for the connections. And then I think back to the degens we have... I could see people using P3 for scanning. Degens.

author: 《VΛ》 KATIREcon Vantarah
content: Here's the problem with market buying and dumping SDUs.  You dont have to own ANY assets to do it.  You dont have to do anything really.   Its like 1% effort w/ zero capital investment.  AND you can easily time the market.  E.g. hold til LP is 15B and then dump.   Where everyone actually participating in the LP system has to design elaborate networks of shipping, freighting, crafting, and upgrading.  And CANNOT time the market because installing takes so long.    Not to mention needing to spend thousands of $$$ to compete.
